meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Portfolio Building to Master Debugging
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to master portfolio building as a freelancer using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress clients.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about knowing every syntax; it's about systemizing your workflow. By building a portfolio-focused project, you force yourself to document patterns, isolate errors using AI-assisted tools like Claude Code, and create reusable components. This transition from 'fixing bugs' to 'building architectures' turns your technical debt into a competitive advantage.
content.intro:
The screen flickers. You’ve been staring at a stack trace for three hours, and the client’s deadline is breathing down your neck. You’re not just stuck; you’re losing money and momentum, trapped in a loop of trial-and-error that yields nothing but frustration. It’s a rite of passage for every developer, but staying here is a choice.

There is a bridge between the chaos of debugging and the calm of professional shipping. By shifting your focus from 'fixing' to 'portfolio building,' you transform these roadblocks into documented assets. Using the precision of Antigravity and the reasoning engine of Claude Code, you can turn your biggest technical headaches into polished projects that demonstrate your expertise rather than your struggle.

steps.title: Define the Scope
steps.description: Narrow down the bug into a micro-feature. By isolating the issue into a standalone module, you make it easier to debug and eventually turn it into a reusable portfolio component.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this code module for architectural flaws and suggest a modular refactor that improves testability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'isolate the current error into a standalone unit test'

steps.title: Automate the Reproduction
steps.description: Don't debug manually. Create a script that triggers the error consistently so you can prove it's fixed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a regression test script that reproduces this specific runtime error consistently.

steps.title: Refactor for Reusability
steps.description: Turn the fixed code into a library or utility function. This adds value to your portfolio and prevents the same bug from recurring.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function into a generic, reusable utility with clean error handling and type definitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file utils/fix.ts --apply 'convert to generic reusable component'

steps.title: Document the Fix
steps.description: Write a case study around the bug. Explain the 'Why' and 'How' for future clients to see your problem-solving process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a technical blog post explaining the logic behind this fix, focusing on performance and scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read --file README.md --append 'Added case study on solving [bug_name]'

steps.title: Deploy as a Portfolio Asset
steps.description: Push the polished, documented code to a public repository to showcase your ability to handle complex technical debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md with installation steps and a usage example for this component.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'git commit and push the refactored code to the portfolio repo'

pro_tips: Treat every bug as a feature request for a better version of your codebase.
Use Claude Code to review your PRs before you commit to ensure no hidden regressions.
Always document the 'Why' behind a hacky workaround in comments; it saves hours of future debugging.
Build a library of 'Golden Components' that you've already debugged and verified.
Focus on shipping small, stable modules rather than large, buggy monoliths to keep your client velocity high.
content.next_steps: Begin by auditing your last three 'impossible' bugs. Apply the refactoring steps above to turn them into your next portfolio showcase piece.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs dictate your workflow and start building your reputation as a high-velocity engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
